name: Pork Chops and Apples in Sweet and Sour Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Center-cut loin pork chops (About 1 1/2-in. thick)
1 Ripe tomato peeled, seeded and cut into 1/4-in cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim most of the fat from the chops.
2. Quarter, core and peel the apples.
3. Heat the oil in a skillet large enough to hold the chops in one layer.
Add the chops and cook until nicely browned on one side, 2-3 minutes.
Turn and cook on the other side about 2-3 minutes.
Continue cooking the chops another 15 minutes, turning them often.
4. Arrange the apple quarters around the chops. Cover closely and cook for 5 minutes.
5. Transfer the chops and apple quarters to a warmed platter and keep hot.
6. Pour off the fat from the skillet. Add the onion and cook, stirring, until wilted.
Add the brown sugar and vinegar and stir. Add the tomato and chicken broth.
Stir to dissolve any brown particles that cling to the bottom and sides of the skillet.
Cook, stirring about 2 minutes.
7. Add any liquid that has accumulated around the chops. Cook until the sauce reduces to about 3/4 cups.
8. Pour the sauce over the chops and apples.
